name: Automation MCP
what_it_does: MCP server providing complete macOS desktop automation capabilities including mouse control, keyboard input, screenshot capture, window management, and UI element interaction for AI assistants.
use_cases: Automated UI testing through AI assistants
Mouse and keyboard simulation for workflow automation
Visual element detection and waiting patterns
not_for: Headless server environments without a display
Security-sensitive environments where desktop control is a risk
Simple scripting tasks where AppleScript alone suffices
best_when: You need AI assistants to have full desktop control on macOS with mouse, keyboard, screenshot, and window management capabilities for complex automation tasks.
avoid_when: You only need basic app scripting (use applescript-mcp instead), need cross-platform support, or are uncomfortable granting Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions to an AI-controlled process.

auth.notes: No application-level authentication. Security relies entirely on macOS permission system (Accessibility and Screen Recording grants). Once permissions are granted, the server has full desktop control.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Fully free and open source with no external service dependencies.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Permission denial errors are documented (directs to macOS System Settings). Beyond that, error handling documentation is sparse. The tool set is comprehensive (20+ tools) but error responses per tool are not documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Mouse clicks, keyboard input, and window operations are inherently side-effectful. No idempotency guarantees - retrying a click will click again.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires Bun runtime (not Node.js) which is less commonly installed
macOS Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions must be granted manually in System Settings
No sandboxing - full desktop control once permissions granted
Coordinate-based interaction is fragile and resolution-dependent
Screenshot capture returns images that consume significant context window tokens
Small project (29 commits) - may have limited edge case handling
Uses nut.js which requires Xcode Command Line Tools on macOS
furi dependency adds another tool to install for the recommended setup path

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
